name: James Burgess
title: Conservation Technologist

contact.availability: Available for conservation technology roles and co-founder opportunities

profile: 14 years building digital products from firmware to cloud. Now redirecting that capacity toward conservation technology — tools that work in the field, not just in a demo.

Track record of walking into technical disaster zones and producing systems that run untouched for years. The same skills that unified fourteen MSSQL servers into one PostgreSQL system, collapsing a week-long ETL to one minute, are ready for the hardest problems in conservation.
PADI Open Water Scuba Instructor with 1,000+ logged dives, 10 specialty instructor ratings, and cleanup dive experience. The ocean is not a hobby — it is the reason for the redirection.
leadership: Full-stack engineering: backend, frontend, firmware, infrastructure, data pipelines, ML integration
14 years shipping production systems at scale — FinTech, actuarial, medical finance (HIPAA), cloud consulting
Led cross-functional engineering teams of 6–8, reporting to C-suite
Designs for field conditions: offline-first, resilient, low-bandwidth, no-internet-operable
Pragmatic builder — "import this" is not a poster; simplicity and durability are moral positions
Open-source contributor to Wildbook (DevOps and infrastructure automation)

projects.name: The Field Company
projects.description: platform for conservation technology that works in the field. Active project: Field Log (offline-first research logger). Vision includes multi-modal mesh network, open ecological database, and 1m² eco health index.

projects.description: open-source ML platform for wildlife photo-ID; contributing DevOps and data pipeline infrastructure

projects.description: offline-first research logger — IndexedDB, camera, GPS. Runs weeks without signal, syncs on reconnection. PWA for conservation fieldwork.
